title: 'No hope for justice': Day after Kejriwal, Sisodia pens letter to Delhi HC judge Swarana Kanta
content: AAP leaders Manish Sisodia and Arvind Kejriwal have declared they will not appear in proceedings before Justice Swarna Kanta Sharma in the Delhi High Court's excise policy case. Citing a lack of hope for justice and potential conflict of interest, they are opting for Satyagraha instead of legal representation.

title: 'Nobody else should be allowed': Gavaskar urges BCCI action over fresh IPL concern
content: Sunil Gavaskar criticised long IPL match durations, blaming frequent on-field entry of reserve players and staff for delays. Despite BCCI penalties, games often exceed four hours. He called such scenes “unnecessary” and urged stricter limits on field access. Gavaskar also stressed discipline, citing Richie Benaud, and suggested reducing batter readiness time and enforcing penalties to speed up play.

title: Assam HS results expected shortly: Steps to download AHSEC Class 12 scorecards here
content: Assam Higher Secondary results for Class 12 are expected today, April 28, at 10:30 AM. Students can access their scores online via official websites using their roll number or name. The provisional mark sheets will be available, with original documents to be collected from schools. This announcement follows exams held between February 11 and March 16.

title: Salesforce, Slack are suing Microsoft over business practice that Google called ‘problematic’
content: Salesforce and Slack have filed a lawsuit against Microsoft in London, accusing the tech giant of anti-competitive behavior by bundling its Teams app with Office. This follows similar complaints in Europe and an ongoing FTC probe in the US into Microsoft's alleged market dominance. Rivals like Google also criticize Microsoft's practices, though Microsoft defends its business model.

title: Internet fumes after Manu Bhaker asked about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: At an NRAI event in Delhi, Manu Bhaker was asked about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, sparking online criticism for shifting focus from her Olympic achievements. Bhaker emphasized mentorship, saying age doesn’t limit talent. Social media, including Joy Bhattacharjya, called the question unfair and cricket-centric. Meanwhile, Sooryavanshi continues to shine, becoming the youngest to 1,000 T20 runs and emerging as a promising future star for India.

title: Trump, Melania call on ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel — what triggered the row
content: Jimmy Kimmel faced backlash for calling Melania Trump an "expectant widow" days before a shooting near the White House Correspondents' Dinner. Melania Trump and Donald Trump condemned the remark as hateful and violent, with Trump calling for Kimmel's firing. White House Press Secretary also criticized the "deranged" rhetoric.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ misses Rs 1780 crore mark worldwide by whisker
content: Dhurandhar 2 experienced a major box office drop on its 40th day. The film earned Rs 1.05 crore in India and Rs 0.25 crore overseas. Its worldwide gross collection reached Rs 1779.02 crore. Competitors Bhooth Bangla and Michael also saw reduced earnings on Monday. The movie's performance reflects a decline after its initial surge.

title: 'I hate all Indian bowlers': Pak batter's controversial remark sparks row - Watch
content: Hasan said he feels extra motivation while playing against India and wants to attack their bowlers. He also said he dislikes every bowler in the Indian team.

title: Samsung Galaxy S26+ review: The ‘Plus Buy’ that holds its ground
content: At a starting price of Rs 1,19,999, the Galaxy S26+ sits in a space where expectations are high. It does enough to justify its position for users who want a large-screen flagship without stepping up to the Ultra model. Though it may not feel like a big upgrade, Galaxy S26+ remains a balanced flagship that gets most things right for everyday use.

title: Salim Dola, key Dawood Ibrahim aide linked to Rs 5,000 crore drug empire, deported to India
content: A major win for Indian security! High-ranking drug mafia operative Salim Dola, a Dawood Ibrahim associate, has been deported from Istanbul. Orchestrating a multi-crore synthetic drug empire, Dola's capture and return to Delhi under heavy guard signifies a significant blow to the D-Company's international network. He is now undergoing intensive interrogation.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: Cowboys send Pickens to Bills for Coleman, picks
content: The Dallas Cowboys face a crucial decision regarding wide receiver George Pickens. CBS Sports suggests a trade. ClutchPoints suggest Pickens could be acquired in exchange for draft capital and wide receiver Keon Coleman. However, recent reports indicate the Buffalo Bills are committed to WR Keon Coleman. This alters the proposed trade scenario and leaving Pickens’ future with Dallas uncertain. But he wants a trade. So, we are back to square one.

title: Fact check: Clavicular pregnancy rumour after 10-day romance debunked
content: Clavicular, whose real name is Braden Peters, went viral after claims he is expecting a child with a girlfriend of 10 days. The rumor came from a viral post and a misunderstood livestream. There is no proof of pregnancy. The story is false, while real concerns remain about his health, recent hospital stay, and ongoing controversies.

title: 'I cried harder' over painful parenting moment with daughter: Serena Williams
content: Serena Williams shared a real parenting moment where she had to discipline her daughter, even though it made them both cry. She stood by her decision after her daughter missed a sleepover for not following bedtime rules. The story shows that discipline is hard but important. Serena Williams continues to balance motherhood with honesty and strong values.

title: ‘Best time to buy a gun… today’: Chilling online trail of suspect behind Trump dinner shooting
content: Social media activity of Cole Tomas Allen, charged in an alleged assassination attempt on President Trump, shifted from gaming to angry political messages. Posts from 2024 and 2025 criticized Trump, questioned election outcomes, and encouraged gun ownership, mirroring a message sent to family before the incident.

title: Putin ally’s $500 million Russian superyacht sails through Hormuz despite US blockade: Report
content: A Russian-linked superyacht, Nord, owned by sanctioned billionaire Alexey Mordashov, has successfully sailed through the Strait of Hormuz. This strategic waterway remains a flashpoint amid ongoing tensions, with maritime traffic significantly reduced. The yacht's passage highlights its defiance of sanctions and the complex geopolitical landscape.

title: King Charles III to address US Congress amid Iran war tensions: What to expect
content: King Charles III will address a joint session of the US Congress, calling for "reconciliation and renewal" in UK-US ties amid strains over the Iran war. The monarch's rare speech aims to reinforce the long-standing alliance, acknowledging political differences while stressing unity against global challenges and shared democratic values.

title: Oil prices climb towards $110: Brent, WTI continue to rise as US-Iran peace talks stall
content: Oil prices surged Tuesday, extending Monday's gains as stalled US-Iran peace efforts and Strait of Hormuz disruptions pressured market sentiment. WTI Crude and Brent Crude saw notable increases, driven by constrained Middle East oil flows and ongoing geopolitical tensions. Investor focus remains on diplomatic developments and central bank policy signals.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock market recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Lupin, Jio Financial Services, Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone are the top stocks that Somil Mehta, Head of Retail Research, Mirae Asset ShareKhan recommends for buying on April 28, 2026.

title: Man stabs 2 security guards near Mumbai; cops probe 'religious' angle
content: A man who returned from the US in 2019 allegedly stabbed two security guards in Thane, Maharashtra, in a suspected religiously motivated attack. The accused, Zaib Zubair Ansari, was arrested within 90 minutes, and the state Anti-Terrorism Squad has taken over the investigation due to his background and recovered incriminating materials.

title: Maharashtra minister Nitesh Rane awarded 1-mth jail for 2019 mud attack on NHAI engineer
content: Maharashtra minister Nitesh Rane received a one-month jail sentence for pouring mud on an NHAI engineer in 2019. The court, while acquitting 29 others, found Rane guilty of intentional insult, emphasizing lawmakers shouldn't take the law into their own hands. His sentence was later suspended for appeal.

title: 'If anyone can challenge Virat, it's ... ': Kohli's record called 'stepping stone'
content: Virat Kohli became the first batter to cross 9,000 IPL runs, further cementing his legacy of consistency and dominance. Irfan Pathan said the milestone is “just a stepping stone” for Kohli. At Arun Jaitley Stadium, Kohli played an unbeaten, match-winning knock. With eight centuries, strong form, and Orange Cap contention, he remains central to RCB’s success this season and their strong playoff push.

title: Sun Pharma buys US-based Organon for $12 billion in biggest-ever overseas pharma deal
content: India's largest pharma company, Sun Pharma, has made its biggest overseas acquisition, buying Organon & Co for $11.8 billion. This deal significantly expands Sun Pharma's global presence and product range. It will double the company's revenue and EBITDA. The acquisition positions Sun Pharma as a major player in women's health and biosimilars. This strategic move strengthens Sun Pharma's position in the global pharmaceutical market.

title: 'MS Dhoni might play if CSK gets out of tournament': Ashwin drops truth bomb
content: Since the start of the tournament, Dhoni has been dealing with a calf injury. He was expected to return for CSK’s last match against Gujarat Titans, but that did not happen.

title: Infosys slips out of top 10 valued companies in country
content: Bengaluru-headquartered Infosys has fallen out of India's top 10 most valued companies, now ranking 11th, while Tata Consultancy Services has dropped to sixth. These shifts have been attributed to artificial intelligence-driven disruptions impacting the business models of major Indian software exporters.  The information technology sector saw significant sell-offs on Monday.

title: Mob lynches Varanasi businessman after his car hits woman
content: A sorrowful episode has unfolded in the Phulpur neighborhood of Varanasi, where a businessman named Manish Singh, aged 36, lost his life in a violent mob attack. The turmoil erupted after Singh's car collided with a woman tending to her household chores.

title: Bengal polls: Shah urges people to vote fearlessly, says forces will stay back for 60 days
content: amit Shah asks people to vote fearlessly without worrying about the "goons" of CM Mamata Banerjee . central armed police forces (CAPFs) have been deployed everywhere . he says a govt will come to office after elections .

title: Foreign secretary may hold 1st bilateral meet with Nepal's Balen Shah govt next month
content: foreign secretary is likely to travel to Nepal next month . the visit could take place on may 11-12 . both sides see the visit as an opportunity to further expand collaboration in multiple sectors . it was proposed when external affairs minister S Jaishankar met his counterpart .

title: Will be back soon for oath ceremony of Bengal’s 1st BJP govt, asserts Modi
content: Rohit Khanna covers sectors like government finance, economy, industry and issues related to financial crime.

title: Marathi issue: Maharashtra puts on hold action against auto, cab drivers
content: Somit Sen, Senior Editor at The Times of India, Mumbai. He covers stories on Power beat in Maharashtra and on Oil & Gas. He also covers RTO, BEST (Mumbai’s public transport buses), transport ministry, Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation, interstate transport (trucks/tempos) and the fleetcabs. Read More

title: Ahead of Amit Shah's visit, Ladakh expands from 2 to 7 districts
content: ladakh's number of districts rose from two to seven after five more were added . lieutenant governor says move fulfils a long-pending demand . the move would "strengthen grassroots governance" and improve service delivery in remote areas .

title: India submits its new climate action pledges to UN body, flags conditions to fulfill promise
content: . Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy. He has been covering Parliament since 2003 to see how politics shaped up domestic policy and India’s position at global platform. Before switching over to explore sustainable development issues, Vishwa had covered internal security and investigative agencies for more than a decade. Read More

title: Bengal polls: Even after SIR, 23 of 142 seats have more woman voters in 2nd phase
content: women voters outnumbered men in 23 out of the 142 Assembly segments going to polls on Wednesday . topping the list is Jadavpur, which now has 11,759 more women voters than men . panihati, where the BJP has fielded the RG Kar victim's mother as its candidate .

title: Royal Bengal battle in the offing as Suvendu takes on 'Didi' at Bhowanipore
content: Subhro Niyogi is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, and his job responsibilities include reporting, editing and coordination of news and news features. His hobbies include photography, driving and reading. Read More

title: Kerala BDS student death: Statewide hartal by Dalit, Adivasi groups disrupts life
content: Protesters blocked traffic, disrupted bus services, and forced shop closures across many areas. In some parts of the state, they also attempted to interfere with the functioning of nationalised banks. Passengers travelling to hospitals were reportedly asked to show medical records to be allowed free passage

title: Why quotas alone won’t increase women’s representation
content: While reservation can address the issue of underrepresentation, its impact is likely to be shaped by existing inequalities related to class, caste etc

title: Assembly election 2026 LIVE: All preparations done for second phase of West Bengal polls, says Election Commission
content: Ahead of the next phase of elections in West Bengal, polling parties have begun moving towards their respective polling stations, including remote riverine areas of Sandeshkhali Assembly constituency in North 24 Parganas district, the Election Commission of India (ECI) said

title: First woman MLA, MP, Minister, CM, PM and President were from Congress, not BJP, says Karnataka Minister Laxmi Hebbalkar
content: BJP misleading nation on women’s reservation to pass delimitation bill, says Karnataka Minister Laxmi Hebbalkar

title: KEM Hospital renaming controversy: Symbolism or Services?
content: A fresh controversy has been brewing with the renaming of KEM Hospital, established on January 22, 1926

title: AAP MLA Mehraj Malik released from Kathua jail after J&K HC quashes his PSA detention
content: Mehraj Malik said now that he had been released, he would continue to raise and talk about the issues of the people

title: An  IT professional turned upcycling artist in Kerala is teaching India to rethink waste
content: Using textile waste and natural dyes, Amal S Pillai’s practice merges design, sustainability and community learning

title: U.S. Pacific Air Forces chief’s visit to India cements defence ties
content: While boosting Indo-Pacific defence ties, a major focus of the visit was advancing logistics cooperation, a statement by the American Embassy says

title: Kerala’s out-of-pocket expense in hospitals still among the highest in country
content: Despite expanded insurance coverage, the expense has remained high in the State, a persistent finding across multiple National Sample Survey rounds for Kerala. Nearly 78% of Kerala’s elderly population reported illness, the highest among States, which could be an indication of its high health-seeking behaviour and higher utilisation of health care and not necessarily worse health outcomes

title: Death of BDS student: Hartal called on April 28 unlikely to disrupt normal life in Kerala
content: Hartal conveners said vehicles would not be forcibly stopped, though they appealed to the public to cooperate by avoiding the use of private vehicles. The clarification came amid concerns over disruption to transport and trade

title: 8th cohort of NSR Pre-doc successfully concludes one-year fellowship at IIM Bengaluru
content: The NSR Pre-doc is designed to create meaningful career pathways for candidates from historically under-represented communities in management and allied disciplines

title: Good prices help Kerala’s Muthalamada mango farmers offset crop losses
content: Most of the 500-plus farmers in Muthalamada, both big and small, made some profit because there was early demand for their mangoes in north Indian markets

title: Jharkhand woman found dead in rented house in Bengaluru
content: The incident came to light after neighbours noticed a strong foul smell emanating from her house and alerted the police

title: India, New Zealand ink “historic” free trade agreement, to be implemented later this year
content: The FTA, negotiations for which were announced in March 2025 and concluded in December 2025, is one of the fastest to have been negotiated by India

title: Tamil Nadu does it again with double-digit economic growth
content: The State registers 10.83% real economic growth rate during 2025-26, according to the recently-released data of the Union Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation

title: Hot Westerly winds set to increase temperatures from May 1 in city
content: However, this rise in temperature in coastal regions will be brought down by the daily onset of the sea breeze that will bring some respite for the coastal areas, says a senior meteorologist

title: Indian-origin man sentenced in Singapore for molesting minor
content: The prosecution had sought 20 to 22 months’ jail for Singh, saying he had exploited a young victim and abused the trust placed in him by the victim and her family members

title: CAPFs go ‘all out’ to win ‘trust’, foster ‘peace’ ahead of last phase of Bengal polls
content: Over 100 armoured and anti-riot vehicles deployed across sensitive areas to instil confidence among voters, says official; security presence has “fostered trust and an atmosphere of peace”, says CRPF

title: Odisha tribal produces skeleton of his sister’s body as proof to claim the money in her account
content: Authorities in the Patna block assured that Mr. Munda would be helped in securing a legal heir certificate and other documents required for claiming the money in the account

title: ‘Basava Sri’ award for H.D. Deve Gowda
content: Mr. Gowda apologised for not being able to participate in the Basava Jayanti programme to receive the award owing to his health

title: M.P. Assembly passes resolution to implement women’s reservation after delimitation amid uproar by Opposition
content: The Opposition members walked out of the House after Speaker Narendra Singh Tomar declined to take up the bill, ruling that a government resolution on the issue — scheduled to be moved by Chief Minister Mohan Yadav — had already been listed

title: Haryana Assembly passes women empowerment resolution; Congress boycotts session
content: Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini said that the constitution of the women empowerment committee would strengthen the women in line with the vision of Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: Citizens, NGOs step in to quench the thirst of urban animals
content: Animal welfare activists in the city have taken initiatives to ensure stray animals have access to water and are raising awareness about it as well

title: No rest for the wicked: Even as Tamil Nadu comes down from its election high, tensions are still rising
content: The issues vary from the advisability of Chief Minister M.K.Stalin taking a holiday in Kodaikkanal to the comparative performance of Tamil Nadu with other southern States with respect to the IT sector to the possibility of a coalition regime getting formed due to the presence of the Vijay-led TVK

title: Bengaluru to host pan-India IIT summit on May 16
content: Themed around ‘Technology Sovereignty’, the one-day conclave is expected to bring together 2,000 participants from across 23 IITs, alongside policy makers, technology leaders, startup founders and investors

title: Residents urge BMRCL to include ‘Bilekahalli’ in IIMB metro station name on Bengaluru’s Pink Line metro
content: The residents have proposed that incorporating both the institutional and locality names would strike a balance between modern infrastructure and local heritage

title: 13 Indian fishermen from Nagapattinam attacked, robbed of their catch mid-sea; one suspected Sri Lankan fisherman nabbed
content: The Sri Lankan fisherman, identified as Ayanthan of  Kankesanthurai, was captured by some Nagapattinam fishermen. He was handed over to the Department of Fisheries

title: Kochi Corporation proposes facilitating nightlife along Marine Drive Walkway
content: Improved lighting and security, a premium cafe, night shops, and an open stage among the facilities proposed

title: 'Why live with him before marriage?' SC says quitting live-in relationship not criminal offence
content: a bench of justices said it could not allow her plea for a criminal case of sexual harassment and exploitation against a woman who was in a live-in relationship for 15 years . the court said there was no legal binding in such a relationship and that people should be careful about the vagaries .

title: South Bengal’s silent voter and the storm it may hide
content: in south Bengal, this has become the default response of many ordinary people . it is not just journalists, but even pollsters seem to face the same predicament . in a tea stall, people bonding even with strangers over why Didi was the best .

title: Amid fresh unrest, Home Secretary reviews Manipur security situation
content: The security vacuum created due to withdrawal of around 8,500 Central armed police forces for deployment during  West Bengal elections, and new areas of conflict between Kuki and Naga communities among the issues discussed

title: Proposal to increase retirement age of judicial officers in Tamil Nadu under consideration: Chief Justice
content: “Most probably, it will be done. It is only a matter of time,” the Chief Justice told senior counsel Vijay Narayan on Monday

title: Internal reservation: Nomadic and micro communities to question latest matrix in Karnataka HIgh Court
content: A meeting of the Federation of Untouchable Nomadic Communities of Karnataka has decided to continue its legal fight to secure a separate category in internal reservation for 59 communities

title: Ladakh groups seek meeting with Shah and steps to build trust
content: Home Minister to visit Union Territory from April 30; activist Wangchuk says govt. should withdraw cases filed against 83 persons held during last year’s protest for Statehood and inclusion in Sixth Schedule; L-G announces creation of five new districts

title: Punjab power outages: SAD blames AAP government’s failure
content: Power Minister Sanjeev Arora, meanwhile, asserted that Punjab remains firmly in control as electricity demand skyrocketed from 7,900 MW to over 12,000 MW within ten days

title: 12-storey-high bridge being built over Sabarmati for bullet train project
content: Foundation and substructure works of bridge over; steps taken to ensure smooth flow of water in the river during construction work

title: Cabinet reshuffle should take place and everyone should get opportunity, says Karnataka Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao
content: Karnataka Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao has said that there are several seniors and experienced leaders in the Congress and they should also get opportunity in the Cabinet reshuffle

title: Kash Patel Not On Cole Allen's Kill List Because He's Hindu? What Report Said
content: Investigators said the manifesto made clear that Allen intended to target top government officials attending the dinner, except for Patel. "Administration officials (not including Patel): they are the targets," the 1,052-word document stated.

title: 14 Killed, 84 Injured After Trains Collide In Indonesia
content: The collision between a commuter train and a long-distance train happened late on Monday in Bekasi, just outside Jakarta.

title: "Economic Nuclear Weapon": Marco Rubio's Hormuz Charge Against Iran
content: Asked if Iran is serious about making a deal, Rubio mentioned the internal turmoil the country is facing as the reason for them want to get out of "the mess they're in".

title: Iran Ready To Reopen Hormuz On 3 Conditions, Trump Unlikely To Accept Them
content: It is not clear why Trump is not satisfied with the proposal, but the Iranian proposal would push negotiations on the country's nuclear programme to a later date. Trump has repeatedly insisted that Iran cannot have nuclear weapons.

title: Trump's Upcoming Events Face Heightened Security After Shooting At Dinner
content: It's the third time in less than two years that a gunman has come uncomfortably close to Trump, renewing the central tension over how to accommodate the public-facing demands of the president's office

title: Microsoft Rolls Out Copilot 365 AI Assistant For Accenture's 7 Lakh Employees
content: Financial details of the agreement were not disclosed by the companies. It is a major boost for Microsoft as just a little more than 3% of its over 450 million 365 enterprise users pay for the $30-a-month offering.
